overview: The task-api has no dependencies and must be built first to provide the backend endpoints. The task-web frontend depends on task-api and is built in phase 2 once the API contract is available.

phases.name: Backend Foundation
phases.description: Build the Go REST API service that provides authentication, task, and category management. This has no dependencies and must exist before the frontend can integrate.

components.name: task-api
responsibilities: Provide authentication endpoints (register, login, logout, password reset) with bcrypt hashing and session cookies
Manage CRUD operations for tasks with filtering, completion toggling, and due-date/overdue computation
Manage per-user categories with unique name enforcement and task reassignment on delete
Enforce strict per-user data isolation via auth middleware and in-memory thread-safe stores
Expose /health endpoint and return structured JSON errors
components.architectureContext: Core backend service on port 9090 consumed by task-web via HTTPS/JSON with credentialed cookies. Owns all domain state (users, sessions, tasks, categories) in memory behind sync.RWMutex-guarded maps.
keyConsiderations: Implement account lockout (3 failures → 15 min, HTTP 423) with per-email attempt counters
Use HttpOnly Secure session cookies with 24h expiry and CORS configured for FRONTEND_ORIGIN with credentials
Structure storage behind an interface so in-memory maps can be swapped for a DB later
Validate inputs (email regex, password≥8, YYYY-MM-DD date, trimmed non-empty strings)
Meet <500ms CRUD latency and 100 concurrent user target via efficient locking and indexed lookups
components.apiContract:
openapi: 3.0.3
info:
  title: Task API
  version: 1.0.0
paths:
  /health:
    get:
      responses:
        '200': {description: OK}
  /auth/register:
    post:
      requestBody:
        required: true
        content:
          application/json:
            schema:
              type: object
              required: [email, password]
              properties:
                email: {type: string}
                password: {type: string}
      responses:
        '201': {description: Created}
        '400': {description: Invalid}
  /auth/login:
    post:
      requestBody:
        required: true
        content:
          application/json:
            schema:
              type: object
              properties:
                email: {type: string}
                password: {type: string}
      responses:
        '200': {description: OK}
        '401': {description: Unauthorized}
        '423': {description: Locked}
  /auth/logout:
    post:
      responses:
        '204': {description: Logged out}
  /auth/password-reset:
    post:
      requestBody:
        required: true
        content:
          application/json:
            schema:
              type: object
              properties:
                email: {type: string}
      responses:
        '200': {description: Email sent}
  /auth/password-reset/confirm:
    post:
      requestBody:
        required: true
        content:
          application/json:
            schema:
              type: object
              properties:
                token: {type: string}
                newPassword: {type: string}
      responses:
        '200': {description: OK}
  /tasks:
    get:
      parameters:
        - {name: categoryId, in: query, schema: {type: string}}
        - {name: dueFrom, in: query, schema: {type: string}}
        - {name: dueTo, in: query, schema: {type: string}}
      responses:
        '200': {description: OK}
    post:
      requestBody:
        required: true
        content:
          application/json:
            schema:
              type: object
              required: [title]
              properties:
                title: {type: string}
                description: {type: string}
                dueDate: {type: string}
                categoryId: {type: string}
      responses:
        '201': {description: Created}
  /tasks/{id}:
    put:
      parameters: [{name: id, in: path, required: true, schema: {type: string}}]
      requestBody:
        required: true
        content:
          application/json:
            schema: {type: object}
      responses:
        '200': {description: OK}
    delete:
      parameters: [{name: id, in: path, required: true, schema: {type: string}}]
      responses:
        '204': {description: Deleted}
  /tasks/{id}/complete:
    post:
      parameters: [{name: id, in: path, required: true, schema: {type: string}}]
      responses:
        '200': {description: OK}
  /tasks/{id}/incomplete:
    post:
      parameters: [{name: id, in: path, required: true, schema: {type: string}}]
      responses:
        '200': {description: OK}
  /categories:
    get:
      responses:
        '200': {description: OK}
    post:
      requestBody:
        required: true
        content:
          application/json:
            schema:
              type: object
              required: [name]
              properties:
                name: {type: string}
      responses:
        '201': {description: Created}
        '409': {description: Duplicate}
  /categories/{id}:
    put:
      parameters: [{name: id, in: path, required: true, schema: {type: string}}]
      requestBody:
        required: true
        content:
          application/json:
            schema:
              type: object
              properties: {name: {type: string}}
      responses:
        '200': {description: OK}
    delete:
      parameters: [{name: id, in: path, required: true, schema: {type: string}}]
      responses:
        '204': {description: Deleted}

phases.name: Frontend Web App
phases.description: Build the React SPA that consumes task-api. Depends on the backend's API contract being available.

components.name: task-web
responsibilities: Provide Login, Register, Password Reset, Dashboard, and Categories pages with responsive layout
Call task-api with credentialed fetch for auth, task, and category CRUD operations
Display tasks with due-date, days-remaining, overdue flagging, and filter controls
Support full keyboard navigation with visible focus and <200ms optimistic UI feedback
Serve a /health endpoint from nginx and support runtime API URL configuration
components.architectureContext: React+Vite SPA served by nginx on port 8080. Sole consumer of task-api, communicating over HTTPS/JSON with session cookies (credentials:'include').
keyConsiderations: Multi-stage Dockerfile (node build → nginx:alpine) and inject VITE_API_URL via build arg or runtime /config.js
Handle 401/423 responses with clear inline error messages and lockout notices
Implement optimistic updates with rollback on failure for <200ms perceived feedback
Ensure accessibility: tab order, Enter submits, Esc cancels modals, visible focus rings
Either configure nginx /api proxy for same-origin or rely on backend CORS with credentials
components.apiContract:
openapi: 3.0.3
info:
  title: Task Web UI
  version: 1.0.0
paths:
  /health:
    get:
      responses:
        '200': {description: OK}
